Football.title: Harry Maguire ‘refused to pay £50k ‘bribe’ to Greek cops to make his holiday arrest go away’ as ace vows to clear name
Football.content:
ENGLAND’S Harry Maguire refused to pay a £50,000 “bribe” to make his Greek holiday brawl ordeal go away, it was claimed last night.

Yesterday the £190,000-a-week Manchester United defender lost a six-year fight to clear his name after a bust-up with police on the island of Mykonos in 2020.





He was repeatedly told paying £50,000 would end the case.

But he vowed to clear his name, with the retrial postponed four times between 2023 and 2025.

It restarted in Syros yesterday where three judges upheld the original verdict and handed him a 15-month suspended sentence.

Maguire, 33 today, branded the decision “a shambles” but said he would appeal to the Greek Supreme Court.



A source close to the player told The Sun: “They offered to make it disappear if he paid £50,000.

“He would rather be found guilty than pay a bribe.

“No apology or payment will ever be made and Harry will clear his name.

“He won’t stop fighting until that happens and will go to the Supreme Court if he needs to.”



The source said lawyers for undercover cops involved in the late-night confrontation had repeatedly offered the out-of-court deal.

They added: “This has always been about paying money.

“He hasn’t paid a single penny and he never will.

“He stuck to his guns and his values and won’t budge.

“For six years they have repeatedly told us that this can be dealt with if he pays £50,000.



“The reason it’s gone this far is because Harry won’t do it.

“All will be revealed in his upcoming documentary.”

Another source said: “The translated statements from the police were primary school standard.

“It was pure guesswork just to figure out what they said had happened.”

Maguire last night started for the Red Devils at Newcastle United.



In August 2020 he was holidaying at a £29,000-a-week villa with wife, Fern, 32, his sister Daisy and six others, including his brother Joe.

The group had spent the evening in the Fabrika district and were waiting for a minibus when Daisy was allegedly approached by two Albanian men.

A confrontation blew up amid claims Daisy, who started drifting in and out of consciousness, had been injected with a date-rape drug.

The group got her into the minibus before asking the driver to take them to a hospital.



Minutes later they were pulled over by eight plain-clothed cops.

A shaken Maguire said at the time: “My initial thought was that we are being kidnapped.

“We got on our knees and put our hands in the air and then they just started hitting us in the legs, saying my career’s over, no more football, you won’t play again.

“At this point I thought there was no chance these are police, I don’t know who they are, so I tried to run away, I feared for my life.

“They didn’t speak.



“They didn’t show us their badges.

“They didn’t do anything.

“They were just plain clothed.

“So I tried to run away.

“I had one hand in the handcuff.



“I was moving my hand.

“This is where the charges have come from — this is what they are saying is resisting arrest and this is what the assault is.

“No punches have been thrown.

“Literally, they are saying I hurt someone’s back because I was ­dragging my arm as I tried to run away because I didn’t know who these guys were.

“I don’t think I hurt him.



“Put it this way: I didn’t hurt him as much as they hurt me.”

One officer claimed Maguire and his brother tried to bribe officers.

Maguire, who categorically denies the allegation, spent two nights in a cell before being standing trial.

His defence team was given just two hours to prepare his case.



He was found guilty of repeated bodily harm, attempted bribery, violence against public employees and insulting behaviour and handed a suspended jail term of 21 months.

Brother Joe, 28, and pal Christopher Sharman, 29, were also convicted.

Maguire launched an immediate appeal which voided the original conviction.

But retrials were repeatedly delayed by the Greek courts — until yesterday.

The Syros appeal court judges convicted him of non-serious assault, resisting arrest and attempted bribery.



Outside court, prosecutor Ioannis Paradisis branded the player “a disgrace to his country and to football”.

He said: “His made-up story was not believed by the court and he was convicted again.

“Throughout he has been totally unrepentant and arrogant.

“We are still waiting for an apology.”

Maguire has been told he will be unable to launch another appeal.



But under Greek law he could be given leave to argue points of law at the higher Supreme Court.

The disastrous court ruling is unlikely to damage his hopes of playing at the World Cup this ­summer.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Those entering the US are required to disclose criminal convictions as part of the visa terms.

But a source said there would be no restrictions.

Football.title: Arsenal player ratings: Gabriel and Hincapie cope comfortably without Saliba as Gyokeres fails to build on Spurs brace
Football.content:
ARSENAL earned another important three points in this nerve-shredding title race.

Bukayo Saka’s deflected strike gave the Gunners an early lead away at Brighton.





Gabriel proved a rock at the back as Mikel Arteta’s side held on for the 1-0 win.

And their mood was boosted even more by Manchester City slipping up with a 2-2 draw at home with Nottingham Forest.

Here’s how SunSport’s Arsenal reporter Simon Collings rated the Gunners players…

David Raya 7

So nearly committed a howler inside three minutes when he gifted the ball to Brighton midfielder Carlos Baleba.

Improved after that and was dominant coming for crosses.

Made a good stop to deny Georgino Rutter in the second half.

Jurrien Timber 7

Carried on from a good performance – and the winning goal – against Chelsea last weekend.

Assisted Saka’s goal and was strong defensively. Starting to look like the player he was earlier this season.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Cristhian Mosquera 6

Brought into the team after William Saliba rolled his ankle during the win over Chelsea.

Booked early on and was almost shown a second yellow just after the half-hour mark.

Composed himself after that before coming off for Riccardo Calafiori with less than 30 minutes to go.

Gabriel 9

Saved Raya’s blushes early on by clearing Baleba’s effort off the line.

That set the tone for a monster performance, where it felt like Gabriel won every header and tackle.



Led the defence so well in the absence of Saliba.

Piero Hincapie 8

Dubbed a “warrior” by Mikel Arteta earlier this season – and you can see why.

Worked tirelessly all game and competed like an animal when the Gunners did not have the ball.



Finished the game at centre-back and was excellent there, too.

Martin Zubimendi 6

Looks in need of a rest, which he should get this weekend when Arsenal head to Mansfield in the fifth round of the FA Cup.

The Gunners finished the game with around 40 per cent possession, which shows how Zubimendi could not control the game.

Declan Rice 7

Guilty of giving the ball away on more than one occasion, but always worked so hard to win it back.

Like Zubimendi, he looks tired and in need of a weekend off. Upped his levels in the second half.



Bukayo Saka 7

Captain for the night on what was his 300th appearance in an Arsenal shirt – and scored what turned out to be the winner inside the opening ten minutes.

The only attacker to look lively for the visitors on an otherwise flat night in front of goal.

Eberechi Eze 5

Still struggling to shine in an Arsenal shirt, unless he is playing Tottenham.



Defensively he does look better, though, and is clearly beginning to understand what is expected of him when out of possession.

Gabriel Martinelli 5

One of two changes and came into the side in place of Leandro Trossard.

A relieved man when his tangle with Brighton right-back Mats Wieffer in the box just before half-time was waved away.

Blunt in attack and completed just three passes.

Viktor Gyokeres 4

A rough night for the striker as he returned to one of his former clubs.



His hold-up play was so sloppy in the first half – and it did not get better after the break. Hooked after an hour.

SUBS

Kai Havertz (Gyokeres 59’) 6

On up front and went close to scoring a second for Arsenal, but Baleba made a good recovery tackle.

Gave the Gunners a focal point and is surely going to be due a start soon.



Leandro Trossard (Martinelli 60’) 5

Immediately into the action with a vital block from Yankuba Minteh’s cross.

He was wasteful at the other end, however, and shanked a good chance wide from Timber’s cut-back.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Riccardo Calafiori (Mosquera 64’) 6

On at left-back, with Arteta seemingly worried about Mosquera getting a second yellow card, as Hincapie shifted to the heart of defence. Steady.

Christian Norgaard (Zubimendi 80’) N/A

Subs not used: Arrizabalaga, Jesus, Madueke, Lewis-Skelly, Dowman

Football.title: Chelsea player ratings: Joao Pedro the complete striker with stunning hat-trick but Reece James struggles in midfield
Football.content:
A JOAO PEDRO hat-trick and a Cole Palmer volley put Aston Villa to the sword as the Blues got revenge on the Villans away from home.

Liam Rosenior made a huge call to drop Robert Sanchez at Villa Park and start Filip Jorgensen instead.





It was a decision that looked bad almost immediately, as former Chelsea target Douglas Luiz opened the scoring inside three minutes with a cheeky flick inside the box.

It was a first half of a few chances, but the in-form Joao Pedro made one count after 35 minutes, tapping home a Malo Gusto cross to make it 1-1.

Aston Villa fans were left outraged before half-time, as Ollie Watkins had a goal ruled out by VAR for his kneecap being offside – chants of “Premier League, corrupt as f***” came from the home support.

With eight minutes added on, Pedro put Chelsea 2-1 up with a delightful chip over Emi Martinez in the Villa net – VAR checked it, but it counted, which was a hammer to the heart of Villa fans.

Cole Palmer added a third for the Blues, celebrating in ice-cold fashion, before Pedro scored his third to make it 4-1, as the Blues wrapped up all three points away from home.

This is how SunSport Chelsea Reporter Lloyd Canfield rated the Chelsea players…

FILIP JORGENSEN – 6

Last time Jorgensen played at Villa Park, it was nothing short of a disaster, so naturally, Rosenior started him here for his first game in months after Sanchez’s poor showing against Arsenal.

It was far from a repeat of that game last season, and after a shaky start he was pretty solid in net for the Blues.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

He made two decent first-half saves to deny Ollie Watkins and then Morgan Rogers, and claimed crosses well too.

MALO GUSTO – 5

Gusto said earlier this week that he wants to become the best in the world in his position – well, he’s got some work to do!

A really poor start to the game was somewhat levelled out by a perfect cross to assist Joao Pedro’s leveller.

Overall he improved in the second 45 minutes as Chelsea grew into the game, but some of his final deliveries could still do with some work.



TREVOH CHALOBAH – 7

Chelsea fans, it’s time to accept that Trevoh Chalobah is the best fit centre-back at the club.

He may be inconsistent at times, but tonight proved why the Blues need to keep him on for next season.

Strong along the ground and in the air, he was better in possession than Fofana next to him, and made a brilliant clearance to deny Ollie Watkins making it 3-2.

WESLEY FOFANA – 5

Returned to the Chelsea backline after his suspension and replaced Mamadou Sarr.



He wasn’t good enough on the ball, and frankly, not good enough off of it either – it was tough watching him try to play a line-breaking pass into the midfield.

Being in the wars was probably the most notable thing about him in this game.

JORREL HATO – 5

Leon Bailey beat him with ease down the Aston Villa right side and crossed as if he was playing with mates in the park for the opening goal.

The 19-year-old seemed to learn his lesson after that at least, and improved defensively as the first half went on.

REECE JAMES – 5

Was guilty of not picking up Douglas Luiz inside the box as he ghosted in and scored the opener for Villa, which looked far too easy.



He will feel hard done by that he didn’t win his side a penalty moments later, after VAR looked at a trip in the box, which wasn’t given.

James, on far from his best night, will be pleased that his good cross set up Palmer’s goal to add the Blues’ third. He looked much more comfortable at right-back than in midfield.

MOISES CAICEDO – 6

Pressed too high for Aston Villa’s opener, and was left in no man’s land as Luiz ghosted in to score.



He did improve, though, after that error, and resumed business as usual in the middle of the park.

His ability to pass a football remains criminally underrated, with some great balls into Palmer and Garnacho that won’t get the credit they deserve.

The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube Calling all Chelsea fans - we have a brand new YouTube channel! The Sun’s Chelsea YouTube channel will be your go-to destination for the latest news, transfer updates, match previews, post-match analysis, exclusive interviews, fan reactions, and breaking stories from Stamford Bridge. Subscribe to The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube now

COLE PALMER – 8

Palmer didn’t look like himself in the first half, and it looked as though it could be another game where he has flashes, with little end product.

The second half, was a different story.

The Chelsea number 10 rifled home Chelsea’s third, and played an excellent pass into Garnacho, who set up Joao Pedro for his hat-trick.



He looked as though he came to Villa Park with a point to prove as he competes with Morgan Rogers to start for England.

I hope you’re watching, Thomas Tuchel.

ENZO FERNANDEZ – 8

Had an impressive first half, with an amazing long ball into Malo Gusto for the Chelsea leveller, and then a deft assist for Joao Pedro’s second.



His passion makes him easy to love for Blues fans, and performances like this make it even easier, doing the simple things well and the complicated things even better.

ALEJANDRO GARNACHO – 7

Was talked up plenty by Liam Rosenior ahead of this game. Having missed out in terms of minutes in the last few, Pedro Neto’s suspension meant he was handed a starting role.

He repaid the faith of the Chelsea gaffer, too, with a neat performance on the left side, always looking to take on his man and create chances – he created five in the first half alone, more than he ever has in a half of football before.

He got what he deserved in the second half, with a lovely assist for Joao Pedro to complete his hat-trick.

JOAO PEDRO – 10

What more can I say about Joao Pedro?



If you think there is a better striker in the Premier League on form, you’ve got another thing coming.

Almost opened the scoring early on with a header, but was denied by Emi Martinez, before tapping home the leveller with 35 minutes played, continuing his excellent vein of form.

Things got even better for JP before half-time, as fans chanted “Pedro again, ole, ole” when he delightfully chipped Martinez to make it 2-1.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Wrapped up his hat-trick in the second half, with a tap-in from close range that took the complete number nine to 14 league goals this season.

Apart from Erling Haaland, if you can name me an all-round better Premier League number nine, please do tell me…

Football.title: Aston Villa 1 Chelsea 4: Joao Pedro inspires crazy comeback with three deadly finishes as Blues close gap on top four
Football.content:
JOAO PEDRO, surely the Premier League signing of the season, stole the headlines.

But this was a night on which almost everything fell into place for Liam Rosenior and Chelsea.





Penalty-spot bully Cole Palmer scored from open play in one of his best all-round displays of the season.

Another much-criticised player, Alejandro Garnacho, was excellent.

A couple of VAR calls went Chelsea’s way at the end of the first half when the score was 1-1.

And even the gamble of dropping goalkeeper Robert Sanchez paid off for Rosenior as the Blues returned to the top five.

Such a positive evening felt a world away when replacement ‘keeper Filip Jorgensen was beaten after less than three minutes.

Douglas Luiz’s goal was his first since February 2024, when he was playing his part in Villa’s qualification for the Champions League.

But it did not prove to be an omen.

Chelsea picked themselves up and produced the best performance of Rosenior’s reign.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

For Villa and Unai Emery, this was another big blow as their one-time title challenge continued to collapse into a scramble to stay in the top five.

Fortune favours the brave and the visitors were worthy winners after Rosenior made the biggest selection call of his two months in charge.

Chelsea ratings vs Villa: Pedro a complete striker with brilliant hat-trick but James struggles in midfield A JOAO PEDRO hat-trick and a Cole Palmer volley put Aston Villa to the sword as the Blues grabbed a sensational revenge win at Villa Park. Liam Rosenior made a massive call to axe Robert Sanchez and hand a rare start to Filip Jorgensen between the sticks. The gamble looked to have backfired almost immediately when former Chelsea target Douglas Luiz opened the scoring after just three minutes. In-form Pedro eventually levelled the scores after 35 minutes by tapping home a pinpoint Malo Gusto cross and then doubled his tally with a delightful chip in first-half stoppage time. Ice-man Palmer then added a third for the Blues after the break, celebrating with his trademark “shrug” in front of the travelling fans. And Pedro completed his clinical hat-trick late on to make it 4-1 and wrap up a dominant three points on the road. Here is how SunSport Chelsea Reporter Lloyd Canfield rated the Chelsea players…

Sanchez had been poor in the weekend’s defeat at Arsenal, yet it was a slightly odd game in which to give Jorgensen another chance to stake a claim.



Because just over a year before, Villa Park had been the scene of arguably the worst moment of the Dane’s limited Chelsea career.

In the 89th minute, Jorgensen allowed Marco Asensio’s shot to creep under his body and seal a 2-1 comeback win for Villa.

Sometimes it is good to face your demons, and Jorgensen had a solid game, making saves and looking more comfortable with the ball at his feet than Sanchez.

But one of his first duties with any part of his anatomy was to pick the ball out of the net. Through no fault of his own, mind.

The blame lay with another stand-in, left back Jorrel Hato, who retained his place because Marc Cucurella was fit enough only for the bench after injury.



Leon Bailey beat Hato far too easily, Ollie Watkins dummied the cross and Luiz, who had started the move, finished it from close range with a lovely flick.

It was a stylish way to score his first goal for more than two years, following a permanent move to Juventus and a disappointing first half of the season on loan at Nottingham Forest.

Chelsea almost hit back immediately, but Emi Martinez saved Pedro’s header.



Moments later, Reece James went down in the box but replays suggested he had run into former Blues player Ian Maatsen, rather than been fouled.

Someone on the Chelsea bench had not got the memo about improving discipline, and was shown a yellow card for protesting.

The vibrant start continued. Palmer forced a save from Martinez, then Watkins and Morgan Rogers called Jorgensen into action.

The visitors were on top, with Garnacho lively, but did not create good open-play chances until their best move of the night produced the equaliser.

Enzo Fernandez found Malo Gusto with a good pass, but the Frenchman’s cross was even better and Pedro scored from close range.



The same combination almost delivered another goal soon afterwards.

Then came two VAR close calls which both went Chelsea’s way.

First Watkins was ruled marginally offside after unnecessarily mistiming his run on to Rogers’ pass.

Then, in the sixth of eight added minutes, Pedro scored again, just about staying onside from Fernandez’s pass before dinking a lovely finish over Martinez.



The stage was set for a compelling second half.

Chelsea have made a habit of dropping points from winning positions this season, while no team in the Premier League had gained more from being behind than Villa.

Not this time.

Palmer played in James down the right and when Martinez could only parry the cross straight out, Chelsea’s No10 rifled the ball home.



The temperature rose a notch when Rogers was booked for kicking the ball away in dissent and Fernandez received a yellow card for clapping referee Jarred Gillett’s decision.

Chalobah celebrated a crucial challenge on Watkins as the Villa man delayed pulling the trigger.

It was breathless stuff, as Gusto wasted a break at the other end before Pedro completed his hat-trick.

It was another fabulous attack as Chelsea moved the ball from front to back with pace and precision.

Palmer found Garnacho with a brilliant crossfield pass and the Argentinian squared it for his fellow South American.



Garnacho should have capped his own impressive night with a goal but could only fire at Martinez from a pass by substitute Cucurella, whose return was another plus for Rosenior.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Former Chelsea striker Tammy Abraham hit the bar in the dying minutes.

And the game had long been over, thanks to the Blues’ current centre forward.

Football.title: Man City 2 Nottingham Forest 2: Pep Guardiola’s men suffer hammer blow in title race as Anderson earns strugglers point
Football.content:
ELLIOTT ANDERSON and Morgan Gibbs White have both featured on Pep Guardiola’s shopping list over the past 12 months.

And here was a painful reminder why the Manchester City boss admires the Nottingham Forest so much.





Gibbs-White was a possible target last summer as Pep looked to find someone to fill the boots of the departing Kevin De Bruyne.

Meanwhile Anderson remains on there – and could yet end up at the Etihad if Rodri or Bernardo Silva are to leave this summer.

As we know, the City boss has great taste when it comes to midfielders.



Both scored second half crackers here to fire a major dent in Guardiola’s title hopes – and secure a huge point for Forest in their survival hopes.

The Etihad boss predicted that the two in the title chase would drop points – he would not have expect his team to let a couple slip here though.

And was this the night we will look back on as being decisive in the title race?

City’s late win at Forest on December 27 looked like a big moment but actually followed by a slump which saw them fail to win any of their next four Prem games.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

But they hauled themselves back into it and their hard-fought victory at Leeds on Saturday made it six in a row in all competitions – and they managed that one without top scorer Erling Haaland.

The Norwegian had to miss the trip to his hometown club after picking up a minor knock in training last week – but he was back for this one.

He pounced on a poor clearance by Mats Sels and City worked the ball into Bernardo Silva but his deflected shot was easy for the keeper.

Phil Foden was back in the starting line-up due to an injury to fellow England ace Nico O’Reilly and his low shot was spilled by Sels but nobody was there to clean up.

Forest have not had the start they were hoping for under Vitor Pereira who had lost his first two games in the Prem before this.



His team were being pegged back in their own half but did have one breakaway as Morgan Gibbs White found space but fired his shot too close to Gianluigi Donnarumma.

The Forest captain was a target for City last summer but in the end they opted for Rayan Cherki and it was the French midfield ace who helped make the breakthrough.

Just after the half hour mark he received the ball from Marc Guehi and with Forest standing off him, he continued to push.

The former Lyon man then whipped over a cross from the right and Semenyo got in front of his man and side-footed a volley that gave Sels no chance.

For the second time in four days, the Ghanaian had put his team ahead – what a signing he is turning out to be for Pep Guardiola.



A flowing City move saw Foden slip in Haaland and the Norwegian went round Sels but the angle was too tight and he could only hit the side-netting.

Then Semenyo rolled a cross right across the face of goal when you’d have put your mortgage on Haaland being there to tap it in.



The second half started in a similar way with skipper Bernardo twisting one way, then the other in the box before seeing a low shot tipped round the post by Sels.

Cherki then blazed an effort over the bar and the home crowd were starting to feel uneasy that their team had not put the game to bed.

Sure enough just over 10 minutes into the second half the visitors were level.

Ola Aina went down the right and crossed to the back post where Igor Jesus did well to nod back into the danger zone.

Gibbs White seemed to have nowhere to go but pulled out the cheekiest of backheels through the legs of Ruben Dias to wrong-foot Donnarumma and find the bottom corner.



Maybe Pep should have signed him after all.

Yet the visitors were on terms for just four minutes – and you might have guessed before the game how it would come.

Amid all the talk of rivals Arsenal scoring from corners, Guardiola said it was ‘good’ that his team were bottom of the table for set-piece goals.

He might think differently now. Rayan Ait Nouri swung over a flag-kick and Rodri charged through a crowd of players to head home.



It was a first Prem goal in 21 months for the Spaniard – who is still waiting for a verdict on a possible ban for misconduct.

The home side looked comfortable and appealed for a penalty as Haaland went down under a challenge from Sels – but ref Darren England was not convinced.

And it proved to be a key moment as Anderson exchanged passes with Callum Hudson Odoi before curling a beauty into the corner from 20 yards.

In a frantic finish, Sub Savinho poked a shot towards goal but it was cleared off the line by Murilo.



Antoine Semenyo opened the scoring in the first half before Morgan Gibbs-White equalised in the 56th minute.

Rodri finished off a corner about 10 minutes later to restore the lead, but a fine Elliot Anderson finish sapped the air out of the Etihad Stadium.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The home side pushed hard to find a winner, but a stubborn Forest team refused to give in despite 10 minutes of added time.

With Forest drawing and West Ham’s win over Fulham, Tottenham are now only one point above the relegation zone, too.

Football.title: Brighton 0 Arsenal 1: Gunners take charge of Premier League title race as Saka’s deflected strike earns crucial win
Football.content:
MIKEL ARTETA promised Arsenal fans to prepare for a thrilling ride.

After the defeat to Manchester United in January, he said: “Jump in this boat, it is going to be fun.”





But there was nothing fun about the Arsenal boat, which docked at the seaside with the players boring the pants off everyone.

However, maybe Arsenal are going to cruise to the title.

Most importantly for Arteta, winger Bukayo Saka celebrated his 300th club appearance to score an early goal which proved to be a winner.

Yet during another painful watch involving the Gunners, who just about did enough, this was proof that the mood music has definitely changed.

They might not be the country’s nastiest, most volatile bunch of supporters down here on the south coast but even Brighton fans cannot stand Arsenal.

This was a night when it was underlined that Arsenal are the most unpopular team in English football, the pantomime villains of the Premier League, the team everyone is now loving to hate.

And other fans are jumping on board with the criticism. Expect this to continue over the next couple of months

The Brighton fans must certainly have read the comments made by their manager Fabian Hurzeler, who complained about Arsenal’s tactics at corners and the amount of time they are taking.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

Arteta had clearly seen those quotes, too. There was some needle between these two in their technical areas.

Each time Arsenal delayed taking a free-kick, there were howls of derision from the Brighton fans. On every occasion David Raya took just too long with a goal-kick, the jeering returned.

The crowd were livid when Arteta called over all 10 of his players while Martin Zubimendi received treatment in the first half.

Arsenal were booed off the pitch at half-time and booed when they returned. When Arteta made his first substitutions of the game, taking over Gabriel Martinelli and the struggling Viktor Gyokeres, and this stopped Brighton from taking a quick free-kick, the entire crowd started calling the Gunners manager a cheat.



But was Arteta bothered or Arsenal’s players? Of course they were not.

They are looking to win the Premier League not a popularity contest and so far, it is just about going to plan.

Arsenal were certainly not great and it took them 63 minutes to even manage a corner. And on this occasion, Declan Rice’s delivery was poor.

But they were leading thanks to Saka’s ninth-minute goal and they protected it fiercely despite the odd scare.



And for Saka, at least this was a memorable evening to celebrate a landmark appearance, which he commemorated with the number 300 embroidered on the back of his shirt.

Over these next couple of months, Saka needs to do a bit more.

This was only his sixth goal and so far, he has managed just three Premier League assists. Compare this to two years ago, when he scored 16 times in the top-flight and delivered nine assists.

Apart from one game against Fulham, Saka has not really delivered his best form this term so now is the time to step it up.

Saka scored with the help of a deflection off Carlos Baleba who wasted an early chance at the other end after a bad mistake by David Raya.



Baleba attempted to scoop the ball over Raya when he should have used power and Gabriel Magalhaes was able to head the ball away for a corner.

Soon after, Baleba got in the way of Saka’s shot although keeper Bart Verbruggen should have done much better because he allowed the ball to go through his legs – even though there was so little pace on the shot.

Equally, home left-back Ferdi Kadioglu could have done more to block Saka’s strike from the edge of the box.



Brighton complained to referee Chris Kavanagh that there had been a clear handball from Piero Hincapie 15 seconds before the goal but it was not deliberate, so VAR was never going to intervene.

That was Arsenal’s only shot of the first half and Brighton were more adventurous even though they struggled to find a way past a visiting defence who were without the injured William Saliba.

Just before half-time, Brighton felt they should have had a penalty when Martinelli wrestled with Mats Wieffer off the ball.

Hurzeler’s team then continued to have the edge in the second half and Georginio Rutter would have scored had he got a better connection to the ball, but Raya still made a decent save.

Yet Arsenal hung on and Havertz should have made it 2-0 late on but Verbruggen saved.



It was not pretty and it was certainly nothing like the “fun” football Arteta had promised.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

In fact, sticking pins in your eyes can be more fun.

But if Arteta wins the title, Arsenal’s fans will not care less about what anyone else thinks

Football.title: Fulham 0 West Ham 1: Crysencio Summerville strikes again to boost Hammers’ hopes of survival
Football.content:
BERND LENO’S birthday balls-up gave West Ham’s survival hopes an almighty shot in the arm.

The Fulham goalkeeper’s second half clanger allowed Crysencio Summerville his seventh goal in ten games to give the Hammers a sensation they’ve not felt for some time: Belief.





West Ham have looked the strongest of those sides fighting to stay up, but it had started to feel as if they had left their turnaround just a little too late.

This was real a chance to pile the pressure on those around them, with Tottenham floundering, Leeds dropping points the night before and Nottingham Forest playing Manchester City.



But coming off and learning of Forest’s point keeping the Hammers in the bottom three on goal difference will have borne frustration.

West Ham’s recent draws with Manchester United and Bournemouth were creditable points but, in their situation, opportunities were still missed – as was the collapse at Chelsea.

Nuno Espirito Santo will know more than any that they could not keep letting these opportunities pass by.

But after a frustrating February, their first outing in March saw the Hammers hold on and deliver.



Nuno shifted to play two up top, bringing Callum Wilson in for midfielder Soungoutou Magassa.

Taty Castellanos, Wilson’s partner, drew a save from Leno in the opening minute – but quietened down from there.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Despite having two strikers on the pitch West Ham struggled to find them, spending most of the first half playing the ball back and forth along their backline.

Fulham – without Harry Wilson due to an ankle injury – were largely unadventurous themselves, though crafted a couple of openings for Joshua King and Alex Iwobi, though neither seriously tested Mads Hermansen.

In the final minutes of the first half, King broke towards the Hammers goal but was stopped by Mateus Fernandes, wiping out the Hammers midfielder in attempting to shoot.



Referee Matthew Donohue – in just his fifth Premier League game – however gave a Fulham free-kick which could have cost West Ham dearly.

Raul Jimenez’s initial effort went behind off of the wall, though Hermansen had to deny King from the following corner.

Staggeringly, Donohue made the same error moments after the restart.

West Ham had threatened with Tomas Soucek seeing a header tipped over the bar, but were soon seemingly in trouble at the other end.

Thomas Cairney shaped to shoot and, just as King had done on Fernandes, kicked the back of a West Ham man – Castellanos.



Donohue pointed to the spot but was quickly corrected by VAR John Brooks, in an example of the technology working as it should.

West Ham went up a level after the break. Castellanos broke through the middle but just saw his ball to Summerville cut out before Wilson came so close to converting at the back post.

Bernd Leno error

Nuno, however, responded by replacing Wilson with midfielder Magassa, while Marco Silva looked to lift his side with Emile Smith Rowe and Oscar Bobb introduced.

Smith Rowe was soon into the action, with a volley at the far post though it lacked the power to cause real trouble.

While Fulham were the ones seemingly pushing – West Ham quickly took the lead thanks to Leno’s wobble on his 34th birthday.



The German goalkeeper raced out of his box to meet a Fernandes hoik upfield.

Leno looked to play to Calvin Bassey but put nothing behind his touch, allowing Bowen to sneak in and intercept.

He quickly fed Summerville, who slipped past two white shirts and swept a simple finish home.

West Ham now needed to hold on, knowing the all too serious cost of another collapse.

Fulham tested their resolve, Iwobi sending a shot whistling over the bar.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Hermansen then came up trumps with a stellar stop, diving low to turn substitute Timothy Castagne’s strike away.

The Dane had a horror start to life at the London Stadium but that could prove a priceless save come the end of the season.

Football.title: Why does Casemiro have a black eye for Man Utd?
Football.content:
Casemiro was seen with a black eye against Newcastle

EYE FOR AN EYE Why does Casemiro have a black eye for Man Utd?

CASEMIRO has taken to the pitch against Newcastle with a distinct new look.

The Brazilian midfielder was seen sporting a black eye during the Red Devils’s clash with Newcastle.



United are pushing for a more comfortable spot in the top four, as Chelsea picked up crucial points behind them.



SunSport can reveal why Casemiro has a black eye.

Why does Casemiro have a black eye?

As of right now there is no explanation as to why Casemiro has a black eye, but it was picked up during the Red Devils’ clash against Crystal Palace.

It is likely that the Brazilian took a bang to the head to cause the black eye.

While the injury was picked up during United’s clash with Crystal Palace, but it remains unclear how.

More to follow as SunSport brings you the latest

Football.title: Arsenal and Man City's next five Premier League fixtures compared after huge title twist
Football.content:
Arsenal and Man City's next five Premier League fixtures compared after huge title twist

Arsenal extended their lead at the top of the Premier League to seven points after beating Brighton 1-0 while Manchester City drew 2-2 with Nottingham Forest

View 2 Images Arsenal and Manchester City's battle for the Premier League title took a major twist on Wednesday evening (Image: Getty)

Arsenal have clinched a significant Premier League title advantage following victory over Brighton whilst Manchester City surrendered points to Nottingham Forest. Bukayo Saka's early goal delivered the Gunners another crucial three points whilst Pep Guardiola's team drew 2-2 at home to Forest.

‌



Antoine Semenyo scored the opening goal for City but Morgan Gibbs-White levelled proceedings. Rodri then put City back in front only for Elliot Anderson to equalise again and help Forest pick up a hugely important point.

‌



Over 250 miles away, a very different game transpired as Arsenal defended resolutely and kept yet another clean sheet. City meanwhile surrendered their advantage on two occasions to drop points and permit Arsenal to stretch their lead at the summit to seven.

‌



With all of that considered, Mirror Football takes a closer look at the forthcoming fixtures for the two championship contenders.

Arsenal move SEVEN points clear as Bukayo Saka sees off Brighton - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Arsenal confirm William Saliba out injured vs Brighton as Mikel Arteta gives update READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Arsenal's next five Premier League fixtures

Everton (H) - March 14.

‌



Bournemouth (H) - April 11.

Man City (A) - April 19.

Newcastle (H) - April 25.

‌



Fulham (H) - May 2/3

Verdict: The FA Cup and Champions League are Arsenal's immediate priorities, but they return to Premier League action just under 10 days later with Everton visiting the Emirates Stadium.

Their away form has been spectacular but will face a stern examination against the league leaders. The Carabao Cup final and the last international break of the season means Arsenal's next league fixture is not until mid-April, with Bournemouth heading to north London.

‌



The blockbuster showdown against Man City follows, with a victory not currently essential for Arsenal to maintain their grip on the title race. The Gunners then return to home turf with Newcastle United heading south, before Fulham later take on Mikel Arteta's side. The Gunners then return to home turf with Newcastle United heading south, before Fulham later take on Mikel Arteta's side.

View 2 Images Arsenal and Man City face off in a huge Premier League clash next month

Man City's next five Premier League fixtures

West Ham (A) - March 14.

‌



Chelsea (A) - April 12.

Arsenal (H) - April 19.

Burnley (A) - April 26.

‌



Everton (A) - May 2/3.

Verdict: Further dropped points from City are entirely conceivable over the coming weeks, with a resurgent West Ham United awaiting them this weekend at London Stadium.

A visit to Chelsea follows before the crunch encounter against Arsenal at the Etihad Stadium, a match they simply cannot afford to lose. Guardiola and his squad then travel to Burnley and Everton, with both sides struggling on home turf of late, making all six points an absolute necessity.

Football.title: Man City surrender Premier League title advantage as reality hits for Pep Guardiola
Football.content:
Man City surrender Premier League title advantage as reality hits for Pep Guardiola

MAN CITY 2-2 NOTTM FOREST: Pep Guardiola's side have handed the initiative for the Premier League over to Arsenal after stumbling at The Etihad

View 2 Images Pep Guardiola now looks unlikely to be lifting the Premier League title this season (Image: Getty Images)

As soon as Elliot Anderson’s sweet strike hit the back of the net, Pep Guardiola dashed to study a monitor next to his bucket seat.

‌



What he watched was a replay of Manchester City’s Premier League title hopes suffering a significant setback. There has long been a suspicion that City have been keeping this Premier League race alive without being anything like the best version of themselves.

‌



And this was confirmation. Guardiola knew it. City just lacked a spark. There has been a lot of talk about how attractive or unattractive Premier League football has become.

‌



You could never put City in the unattractive bracket but you could occasionally put them in the predictable bracket. But that predictability normally has a lot to do with the opposition’s set-up. Content not to have possession, a block of the lowest order, wait for the odd counter-attacking titbit, force Guardiola’s side to pick its way through a defensive mesh.

And that can be painstaking. It certainly was for half an hour against Forest, the clearest chance falling on the breakaway to Morgan Gibbs-White, who decided it was a choice between precision and power but chose neither.

FOLLOW OUR MAN CITY FB PAGE! Follow our Manchester City Mirror Football Facebook page

‌



Arsenal move SEVEN points clear as Bukayo Saka sees off Brighton - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Liverpool receive Champions League boost as Galatasaray fans BANNED from clash READ MORE :

That is probably because the Forest captain is not playing with the sort of confidence that is coursing through Antoine Semenyo’s veins.

Semenyo has many attributes but, considering his record, his predatory qualities are perhaps a little under-rated. He is a thoroughbred footballer and a fox in the box. His finish from Rayan Cherki’s assist was of Sergio Aguero style and quality. It was innovative, it was instinctive.

It was a moment of ingenuity this game badly needed. Forest’s ultra-cautious approach was, of course, not unexpected, but this is a City side that can be ruffled. Guardiola believes Rodri will not be at his best for some time and, on this evidence, he is right.

‌



Forest had joy in the centre of midfield and the move for the equaliser - finished by a fiendishly clever back-heel from Gibbs-White - emanated from that area.

In the immediate aftermath of the Forest goal, City looked spectacularly vulnerable but they went back in front with a goal Arsenal would have been proud of. Erling Haaland won the corner and then distracted Forest keeper Matz Sels, allowing Rodri to head in from close range.

View 2 Images Rodri reacts to Man City's draw against Nottingham Forest (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Who will win the Premier League title? Share your predictions in the comments below

It was a topical way to score what seemed to be an important goal. But its importance was diluted by Anderson’s hit, which was sweet but was facilitated by some very slack pressing on City’s part, on Phil Foden’s part, in particular.

It was no surprise that Foden immediately made way for Jeremy Doku. But the damage had been done and, if anything, Forest looked the team more likely to go on and produce a winning goal.

‌



In keeping with the theme of the week, a set-piece looked the most likely source of a decisive goal and both sides created half-chances in the dying stages.

But with the last dead-ball hit of the game, Semenyo’s right-footer just missed its mark and City’s hopes of another title slipped a little further away.

Football.title: Arsenal master the dark arts again to take massive step towards Premier League title
Football.content:
Arsenal master the dark arts again to take massive step towards Premier League title

BRIGHTON 0-1 ARSENAL: Bukayo Saka's first-half goal was the difference as the Gunners took a huge step towards their first Premier League title in more than 20 years

View 3 Images Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta talks to his players during an extended break

They have become the pantomime villains of the Premier League. Arsenal are the team everyone loves to hate.

‌



But somehow - through sheer grit and determination - they ground out another hard fought win to strengthen their grip at the top of the Premier League table.

‌



It was ugly, scrappy and yet Arsenal left the stage at the AmEx to rounds of jeers and boos - but also with a precious three points to push them seven clear at the top after Manchester City slipped up.

‌



No wonder Arsenal’s players celebrated with the travelling fans at the end because this felt like a huge night in the title race as the pendulum swung firmly in their favour with just eight games left.

Bukayo Saka’s ninth minute goal was all that it needed as Arsenal held on and clung on for a precious victory. They were wind-up merchants as they did not care about running down the clock, playing for tactical fouls and using all the dark arts.

Arsenal move SEVEN points clear as Bukayo Saka sees off Brighton - 5 talking points READ MORE :

William Saliba injury update as Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta explains absence vs Brighton READ MORE :

But it was enough to get the job done even if is not very easy on the eye to say the least and quite how Brighton failed to score is anyone’s guess but also testament to Arsenal’s resilience. Gabriel was a man mountain in the Arsenal defence while Piero Hincapie also scrapped and fought for every ball. It was that sort of game.

‌



The title race has been nip-and-tuck between Arsenal and City and it has been rare for them to actually kick off on the same day and at the same time.

Incredibly, this was the last trip out of London for Arsenal in the Premier League for the rest of the season other than next month’s potential title crunch at City. Arsenal also play before City in their next three match rounds so it will be a test of their title mettle in the gripping head-to-head.

It certainly felt like a tougher test for Arteta’s men with their trip to Brighton - and they started like a team who looked nervous as they knew it was a big night for their title ambitions.

‌



View 3 Images Arteta's side moved seven points clear at the top of the Premier League

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Gunners keeper David Raya’s sloppy pass out from the back went straight to Brighton midfielder Carlos Baleba who had a clear run on goal. Baleba chipped the ball over Raya and Gabriel heroically tracked back to head off the line. It was a huge let-off.

‌



Arsenal then got a very lucky break before going ahead. Brighton screamed for a handball against Piero Hincapie which was not given. Arsenal played on and there was 15 seconds between that offence and the visitor’s opening goal. It is questionable whether VAR would pull the play back.

Jurrien Timber fed the ball wide to Bukayo Saka and his shot took a horrible deflection off Baleba which wrong footed Bart Verbruggen but the Brighton keeper still should have done better.

Arsenal still looked edgy. Cristhian Mosquera was in for the injured William Saliba and he gave the ball away before being booked for a foul on Georginio Rutter. Gabriel bailed Arsenal out again with a last-ditch tackle on Karou Mitoma.

‌



View 3 Images Bukayo Saka scored the only goal as Arsenal beat Brighton

Arsenal were second best and up against it. Brighton got increasingly frustrated as the fans jeered every decision, screamed at Arsenal’s time-wasting and tactical fouls.

Arteta’s men have become so unpopular with every other set of fans. Brighton had big chances as Yankubah Minteh cut inside the box and somehow his low cross did not end up in the net.

‌



Rutter forced a great save at full stretch from Raya. Mats Wiefer and Jack Hinshelwood both missed headed chances. Incredibly, Arsenal - the set-piece kings - did not win their first corner until the 63rd minute. They created very little.

Arsenal’s former Brighton forward Leandro Trossard had one big chance after coming on but steered his effort well wide to the delight of the home fans.

The visitors threw on Kai Havertz who at least gave them more quality up front than Viktor Gyokeres. Havertz’s best effort forced a good near post save from Verbruggen.

Football.title: Arsenal move SEVEN points clear as Bukayo Saka sees off Brighton - 5 talking points
Football.content:
Arsenal move SEVEN points clear as Bukayo Saka sees off Brighton - 5 talking points

BRIGHTON 0-1 ARSENAL: The Gunners have moved closer to claiming the Premier League title after just about finding a way past Fabian Hurzeler's side on Wednesday afternoon

View 3 Images Arsenal now sit seven points clear at the top of table (Image: Getty Images)

Arsenal have extended their lead at the top of the Premier League table to seven points after beating Brighton.

‌



Bukayo Saka gave Arsenal the perfect start as his deflected effort found the net. David Raya produced a brilliant stop to keep out Georginio Rutter in the second half.

‌



It was an attritional game from then on, with Mikel Arteta's side forced to defend to close out victory. But close out victory they did, with the win seeing them extend their advantage at the top of the table to seven points, with Manchester City failing to get over the line against Nottingham Forest.

‌



Here are all of the talking points from the Amex Stadium:

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Mikel Arteta tells fans to stop watching Premier League if they want to be entertained READ MORE :

Mosquera struggles in Saliba absence

Arsenal were hit with some bad news before kick-off, with William Saliba succumbing to an ankle injury he picked up against Chelsea. He was left out of the squad entirely.

‌



Cristhian Mosquera was named in his place but it was a difficult evening for the Spaniard. He was booked early on for a foul on Georginio Rutter, while he might have felt fortunate to have got away with another mistimed challenge on the Frenchman.

He was eventually replaced just after the hour-mark by Riccardo Calafiori.

View 3 Images Cristhian Mosquera replaced Saliba in the Arsenal starting XI (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Raya let off the hook

With Saliba out, the onus really was on Gabriel to help his fellow defenders through the game. And he did that in the opening few moments as goalkeeper Raya made an uncharacteristic error.

In attempting to play out from the back, he presented the ball straight to Carlos Baleba, who went for an audacious chip. Luckily for Arsenal, Gabriel had the foresight to stand on the goalline and was in the right place to head away.

Who was man-of-the-match between Brighton and Arsenal? Share your thoughts in the comments below

‌



Saka brings up 300

While Baleba could have played a key role in an opening Brighton goal, he then gave the Gunners a helping hand. With Saka - who was making his 300th appearance for Arsenal - given far too much space down the right flank, the England man cut inside and set his sights on goal.

His effort took a touch off of Baleba, beating Verbruggen all ends up. The goal represented Saka's ninth in all competitions this season, having also netted at Wolves last month.

England boss Thomas Tuchel will certainly be delighted with his recent form as he attempts to pick his World Cup squad over the coming months.

‌



View 3 Images Bukayo Saka celebrates his goal vs Brighton (Image: Getty Images)

Chances come for Brighton

Arsenal were certainly made to work for their victory against Brighton, with the Seagulls having had a number of chances.

The best of those arguably fell to Rutter, who saw his curling effort just about parried away by Raya. Mats Wieffer also had a wonderful opportunity to nod home but could not get enough power into his header to beat the Spaniard.

‌



Advantage to Arsenal

It's not very often that Manchester City and Arsenal will be playing at the same time between now and the end of the season. So news from the Etihad will have been celebrated by those in the Arsenal dressing room at the Amex.

The Gunners are now overwhelming favourites to claim the title. Of course, the momentum could still swing, with Guardiola's side having a game in hand, while the two teams are also set to come together. But Arsenal certainly have an overhwhelming advantage as it stands.

Football.title: Man United handed instant penalty verdict moments after red card fury against Newcastle
Football.content:
Jacob Ramsey was sent off for Newcastle United against Manchester United for diving in stoppage time of the first-half.

The Magpies midfielder was shown a second yellow card when he went down inside the box after pushing the ball past United goalkeeper Senne Lammens. Lammens came rushing off his goal line and Ramsey got a decisive touch around him.

The former Aston Villa player went to ground, and referee Peter Bankes adjudged the 24-year-old to have feigned contact with Lammens. He showed Ramsey a second yellow card in what was a hugely controversial moment in the Premier League clash.

Ally McCoist said on co-commentary on TNT Sports: "It's an interesting one. Initially, when he goes down, I wasn't sure he was looking for it.

"He knocks the ball by him, I'm not sure it's a real effort to con the referee, to be honest with you. I know he goes to ground, but there's no way he's giving a penalty.

"Is he just trying to get out of the road? Is he off-balance? That's a really interesting one. Does he touch him there, and it just knocks him off balance a little bit?"

While former Newcastle goalkeeper Shay Given said: "I think it's extremely harsh." Owen Hargreaves, the ex-Manchester United midfielder, also questioned the decision.

Hargreaves said: "I think because he doesn't go down when the keeper is out, he takes a step, takes a second and then just collapses. I think that's what they've seen, but I think it's harsh as well."

Ramsey was first booked for pulling back Casemiro. "Why's he getting booked so early?" said Given at the time. "It's a needless foul on the halfway line, there's no danger, so I think that's an easy decision."

Image: Ash Donelon/Getty Images) Ash Donelon/Getty Images)

Not long after Ramsey was sent off, Newcastle were awarded a penalty when Anthony Gordon was brought down by Bruno Fernandes. Fernandes tripped Gordon inside the box and the England winger converted from 12 yards.

Given said: "This is a much easier decision for Peter Bankes, he took his time but you seen Sandro Tonali was adamant and then he's convinced himself. Bruno Fernandes was pleading innocence but I think he'll see from the replay it was a clear penalty, knee on knee, it's a penalty for me."

Hargreaves agreed, adding: "Yeah. It's a penalty." Casemiro then equalised for United to end a crazy spell of first-half stoppage time.

Bankes was subjected to fierce boos at half-time at St James' Park, with the home crowd furious at the sending off. The referee was also jeered as he came back onto the pitch for the second half.

Football.title: Kai Rooney and JJ Gabriel send clear message to Man Utd owner Jim Ratcliffe
Football.content:
Kai Rooney and JJ Gabriel send clear message to Man Utd owner Jim Ratcliffe

Kai Rooney is enjoying a productive season within the youth ranks at Manchester United as he attempts to follow in his Dad’s footsteps alongside talented teammate JJ Gabriel

View 2 Images Rooney netted a goal in extra-time against West Ham (Image: Getty Images)

Kai Rooney has underlined his potential once again, having guided Manchester United into the Premier League Cup final in front of a watching Jim Ratcliffe. The youngster has been a part of the Red Devils’ youth system since the age of 11 as he attempts to follow in his iconic dad’s footsteps.

‌



And he took a considerable step forwards in doing that this week, coming off the bench for the under-18 side to score in extra-time against West Ham.

‌



Fellow wonderkid JJ Gabriel was also on the scoresheet in extra-time. Co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe was present in Carrington to watch the game, having taken an interest in the academy since his minority takeover.

‌



His private jet was also on the premises, with the first-team due to take on Newcastle away from home on Wednesday evening. Ratcliffe is not the first big name to watch Rooney in action over the past week.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Kai Rooney found solution to Brooklyn Beckham issue after David and Wayne influence READ MORE :

Dad Wayne was also in attendance last week to take in United’s 6-1 win against Derby County. Having initially been named on the bench, Rooney junior managed to grab himself an assist in the rout.

‌



The fact that Wayne was in attendance was certainly a surprise, having previously been banned. Speaking in 2024, wife Coleen said: "That's the thing with kids I think, it's hard to go on days out. Kai told Wayne to stop coming to football games, when he played grassroots tournaments and stuff because he used to get swarmed and he couldn't even watch the game.

"How do you say to all of these kids, 'Go away, I'm watching my son?' Adults, it's different, you could speak to them. So he (Kai) just said, 'Oh, dad there's no point you coming because you don't even watch me play anyway.'

View 2 Images Rooney has made waves with the under-18s this season (Image: Getty Images)

‌



“Which is sad, but it can't be helped anyway. At the same time, the fans help you along the way and get you where you are."

Rooney has previously offered his take on the United academy and claimed that the club’s young players had more pressure on their shoulders following Cristiano Ronaldo’s return.

He said: “There's a bit more pressure on them as well. Kai and [when Cristiano] Ronaldo came back, Ronaldo's lads and Michael Carrick's lad were all on the same team.

Article continues below

"You go and watch and then all of a sudden there's like 10 times the amount of people that's normally there, all around the pitch watching, so there's a lot more pressure because they're coming to watch the three kids to see how they play and stuff. They've got to be ready."

Football.title: Liam Rosenior sends Robert Sanchez clear message as Chelsea keeper dropped vs Aston Villa
Football.content:
Liam Rosenior sends Robert Sanchez clear message as Chelsea keeper dropped vs Aston Villa

After he struggled in Chelsea's 2-1 away defeat against Arsenal, Robert Sanchez found himself on the bench for the Blues' away match against Aston Villa in the Premier League

View 3 Images Robert Sanchez struggled for Chelsea in their defeat to Arsenal (Image: James Gill - Danehouse)

Liam Rosenior has warned Chelsea goalkeeper Robert Sanchez there is no No. 1 goalkeeper in his eyes. The Blues manager chose to deploy Filip Jorgensen for the Blues' crucial away clash against Aston Villa.

‌



Rosenior, 41, is the third Blues boss to drop Sanchez for a Premier League match after Mauricio Pochettino and Enzo Maresca did so in similar fashion. Sanchez has enjoyed one of his best seasons at Stamford Bridge, but he struggled under the pressure of Arsenal's set-pieces in the west Londoners 2-1 away defeat to the Gunners.

‌



The ex-Brighton and Hove Albion goalkeeper misjudged an attempt to claim a corner in north London, which resulted in Jurrien Timber heading in from close range. A matter of days after the Spaniard's mistake, he found himself on the bench at Villa Park.

‌



Nine stars who could replace Harry Maguire at World Cup including Chelsea ace and 6ft 7' giant READ MORE :

Speaking to TNT Sports before the match, the Chelsea manager explained: "He's (Jorgensen) played very good in training. The way I work with goalkeepers, I don't have an outright number one.

"I want competition in every area. He's been waiting very patiently for his chance. Now I've given it to him this evening."

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Rosenior had defended Sanchez before the match. The Englishman said: "I think each player contributes to the overall performance and functionality of your team. So yes, the first few minutes we had a few nervy spells.

"The fact that we played out from the back affected their press and gave us space in the areas that we wanted to have those spaces. I think that's just as clear.

View 3 Images Sanchez got caught out of position for Arsenal's winning goal (Image: Stuart MacFarlane, Arsenal FC via Getty Images)

‌



"So I don't want to single out players and say what they can or can't improve. It's about the functionality of the team. I don't think anyone can say in the moment, other than the two set play goals, my team did not function well in the game. By the way, the league leaders, top of the Champions League.

"We functioned in a very good way for a lot of the game. The reason we lost the game was we didn't take care of the moments. Nothing to do with Rob's build, nothing to do with the way that we played. We have to manage those moments to win games of football."

Then, discussing Aston Villa, who have netted more goals from distance than any other side in the Premier League this season, Rosenior said: "You would never tell Moises Caicedo or Enzo [Fernandez] or Reece [James] or Cole [Palmer] or Pedro [Neto], you would never tell them not to take a shot unless they didn't think it was the right decision in that moment.

‌



View 3 Images Jorgensen was selected to faced Aston Villa (Image: NurPhoto, NurPhoto via Getty Images)

"In terms of preparing for Aston Villa, the reason they score great goals is because they have great attacking players. We show individual clips to our players. Our players know the strengths and weaknesses of each individual we're up against. It's going to be a very difficult game.

"They're very clinical in terms of the xG that they create, in terms of the values of the goals that they score. They're a good team.

Article continues below

"It's going to be tough because they're going to have a reaction from their last result. We have to make sure we're prepared for a very difficult game."

Football.title: Harry Maguire conviction explained - can he play for Man Utd? England World Cup visa issue
Football.content:
Harry Maguire conviction explained - can he play for Man Utd? England World Cup visa issue

Greek courts have found Harry Maguire guilty of attempted bribery, non-serious assault and resisting arrest, handing the Manchester United player a 15-month suspended sentence

View 3 Images Harry Maguire can still play for Manchester United (Image: Getty Images)

Harry Maguire faces a 15-month suspended sentence after being found guilty of attempted bribery, non-serious assault and resisting arrest. A retrial at the fifth attempt took place on Wednesday, approaching six years after his arrest on the Greek island of Mykonos.

The sentence, which had been 21 months, was reduced to 15 months and 20 days. Maguire, 32, has always denied any wrongdoing, and his legal team plans to appeal the verdict after these proceedings on the island of Syros.

Mirror Football explains the conviction and what it means for Manchester United, England, the World Cup and much more below...

Can he play for Man United after the guilty verdict?

Maguire can still play for United because there is no immediate enforcement of a suspended sentence in terms of having to go to prison. The defender is due to be in the squad for their Premier League match against Newcastle United at St James' Park just hours after the hearing, as he has started seven straight games since Michael Carrick was appointed head coach, through the end of this season.

United are yet to comment following Wednesday's verdict. His current contract, which the club extended by an additional year last January, is due to expire this summer.

View 3 Images Harry Maguire will appeal the conviction again (Image: Alex Livesey/Getty Images)

How does conviction affect World Cup travel?

Maguire will have to declare the conviction when applying for a visa. He has to obtain a court record detailing the nature of the offences committed or currently pending, the sections of law contravened (if relevant), and the penalty imposed.

He may also require a waiver of ineligibility to travel to the United States, as convicted individuals can be permanently ineligible to receive a visa. If that is the case, waiver applications can take up to nine months, and the World Cup is less than 100 days away.

Maguire, however, did attend United's pre-season tour in the United States last summer. One has to declare arrests, even if they do not lead to convictions, so he will have had to do so previously.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

What exactly is Maguire charged with?

Maguire was found guilty of attempted bribery, non-serious assault and resisting arrest. The severity of those charges have reduced since his initial sentence, which was for attempted bribery, repeated bodily harm and violence against public employees and insult.

He spent two nights in custody at a police station after being apprehended. During that time, Maguire allegedly told officers: "Do you know who I am?"

His incarceration followed an incident involving two Albanian men and his sister, Daisy, while they were waiting for a taxi to take them back to their villa. Maguire claims they injected her with an unknown substance that caused her to lose consciousness.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Has Maguire had to pay a fine or serve any prison time?

As part of Maguire's original sentence, he faced a €1,500 fine, which converted to just over £1,300; however, there is no such penalty in the revised sentence after they reduced its severity. He is also said to have refused several chances over the years to settle the case with a financial offer.

Maguire is not due to serve any time in prison at this stage, as the sentence is suspended, much like the original one from 2020, due to it being a first offence and the charges being misdemeanours, as Greek courts can suspend custodial sentences of up to three years.

Why was the case delayed for six years?

The case is only having another hearing now because of its previous four postponements before the retrial finally took place at a fifth attempt in Syros. The absence of translated court documents into English led to the most recent postponement in October last year, a representative of the police officer involved, Jean-Jacques Paradissis, told Sky Sports News.

He said that "the court translator has not yet translated the documents Maguire's team requested into English". However, Paradissis expressed confidence last autumn that the case would conclude this year before the Statute of Limitations expires in 2028, eight years after his initial arrest.

View 3 Images Maguire attended court in Greece in 2020 (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Why was Maguire not in court?

Maguire did not attend the hearing in person because there was no requirement for him to do so. The date also fell on the same day that United play Newcastle at St James' Park in the Premier League.

He was also not present for the original verdict six years ago, again because he did not need to be in attendance. Maguire was United captain at the time and a key member of the England squad under Gareth Southgate, having been selected for Nations League matches against Denmark and Iceland hours before the initial sentencing in the summer of 2020.

Football.title: Joao Pedro hat-trick for Chelsea blows Villa away
Football.content:
Joao Pedro scored a hat-trick as Chelsea came from behind to dismantle Aston Villa 4-1 at Villa Park and press their claims for Champions League qualification.

Douglas Luiz's early goal might have been expected to settle Villa's nerves after their recent poor form but his Brazilian compatriot scored twice before the interval before Cole Palmer doubled Chelsea's advantage early in the second half.

When Joao Pedro completed his hat-trick there was no way back for Unai Emery's side. Villa could point to Ollie Watkins' goal being disallowed for offside in the first half but in truth Liam Rosenior's side played the slicker stuff throughout and deserved the big win.

The result moves Chelsea above Liverpool into fifth spot, now just three points behind an Aston Villa's side that remain fourth but find themselves on a downward trajectory after one win in their last seven games. The race for the Champions League heats up.

Player ratings Aston Villa: Martinez (6), Cash (6), Konsa (4), Mings (5), Maatsen (6), Onana (5), Luiz (6), Buendia (5), Rogers (6), Bailey (6), Watkins (5).



Subs: Bogarde (4), Alysson (6), Barkley (6), Sancho (6), Abraham (6).



Chelsea: Jorgensen (7), Gusto (7), Chalobah (7), Fofana (6), Hato (7), Caicedo (7), James (7), Enzo (8), Palmer (8), Garnacho (8), Joao Pedro (10).



Subs: Lavia (6), Cucurella (7), Adarabioyo (6), Delap (n/a), Andrey Santos (n/a).



Player of the Match: Joao Pedro.

Player of the Match: Joao Pedro

Image: Joao Pedro looks on after giving Chelsea a 2-1 lead at Villa Park

Even prior to this performance, Joao Pedro had equalled his best goal tally in a league season, matching his total of 11 for Watford in the Championship. This hat-trick moves him onto 14 Premier League goals for Chelsea. He was absolutely superb.

The Brazilian forward's classy hold-up play and ability to drift around the pitch means he is often characterised as a No 10 as much as he is a No 9 but what was striking about this performance that did for Aston Villa was his movement inside the penalty box.

Elusive in the area, these were the goals of a poacher. And his finishing just oozed quality. While Ollie Watkins struggled to show his best form in front of goal at the other end of the pitch, the contrast was marked. Chelsea's difference-maker up front.

Team news Unai Emery made three changes to the Aston Villa team that lost at Wolves with Tyrone Mings, Ian Maatsen and Leon Bailey returning.



Robert Sanchez was dropped by Chelsea. Malo Gusto also with Reece James moving into midfield, while Alejandro Garnacho started.

Chelsea show their true quality

Even in the defeat against Arsenal, Chelsea hinted at their potential for long periods, matching the Premier League leaders and even outplaying them on their own patch for a period in the second half. Details and ill-discipline have undermined them too often.

"This game is decided on moments," Rosenior had said before kick-off. He was decisive himself in dropping Robert Sanchez after his poor showing against the Gunners, bringing in Filip Jorgensen who did well. His players were decisive for him on the pitch.

Joao Pedro was the star but when Chelsea are flowing and the confidence is up, this young team have the makings of something special. A run of three winless games brought to an end in emphatic fashion. Now they must make it count and press on.

Villa's season starting to unravel

Aston Villa have been wobbling for a while but never this season have they been torn to shreds quite like they were by Chelsea. Joao Pedro's movement was too good for them in the first half and Emery's high line was utterly exposed in the second.

Chelsea's expected goals total of 3.92 is the highest that Villa have conceded in a Premier League home game under the Spaniard and the highest under any manager since a defeat to Leicester back in 2019. On a huge night for the team, they crumbled.

There is still time to salvage a successful season and Emery's impassioned defence of the campaign after the defeat to Wolves on Friday still rings true. They are fourth and among the favourites to win the Europa League. But this poor form cannot be ignored.

Football.title: 'A miracle to walk' - Pavan discharged from hospital after lift shaft fall
Football.content:
Italian golfer Andrea Pavan has been discharged from hospital days after falling down a lift shaft in South Africa.

The 36-year-old fell three floors after stepping through open lift doors into an empty shaft while staying in Stellenbosch ahead of the South African Open.

Pavan - a two-time DP World Tour winner - was rushed to a local hospital, where he was found to have significant injuries to his shoulder and back, including severe shoulder damage and fractures to several vertebrae.

He underwent back and shoulder surgery but has been deemed fit enough to leave hospital less than a week after the freak accident.

'A miracle to walk'

"Today was a big day. I got discharged from the hospital to continue my rehab at an accommodation nearby kindly set up by the Rupert family," Pavan wrote on Instagram.

"It truly feels like a miracle to be able to walk and start to do some basic activities, although at a very slow and careful pace.

"I would like to thank all the Mediclinic staff - all the surgeons, nurses and everyone who has made this incredible recovery possible.

"Thank you to DP World Tour and Sunshine Tour, in particular Mateo, Ben, Joel and all the people who helped me over this last week. The DP World Tour players and caddies who came to visit me in ICU while playing the SA Open - it truly felt like a big family!"

Image: Andrea Pavan is a two-time winner on the DP World Tour

Former team-mate Matt van Zandt set up a GoFundMe page to support Pavan's wife Audra and their three children with medical expenses.

It has seen professional golfers such as Richard Mansell, Nacho Elvira and Jhonattan Vegas donate, with over £61,000 raised.

"Thank you to all the friends, family, sponsors and people who reached out with messages and prayers and stepped in to help in all kinds of different ways," Pavan added. "I am sorry I have not been able to get back to all of you yet, but my heart is overwhelmed with love and joy.

"God has answered many prayers; it truly feels like His hand is on my recovery."

Despite his release from hospital, Pavan revealed he faces a five-week recovery period in South Africa before he is able to return to the United States.

"The road is very long, but this small step feels very good! I will need to stay in South Africa another five weeks to rehab both shoulder and back, heal other bruises and secondary injuries, and once back home continue the rehab journey."

Football.title: Arsenal edge Brighton to go seven points clear
Football.content:
Arsenal ground out a 1-0 win over Brighton to capitalise on Manchester City's slip-up against Nottingham Forest and move seven points clear at the top of the Premier League.

As their title rivals were held to a 2-2 draw at the Etihad Stadium, Bukayo Saka's deflected, eighth-minute strike proved decisive for the Gunners in a tough encounter on the south coast.

Saka's strike, which wrong-footed Bart Verbruggen after taking a touch off Carlos Baleba, came from one of only two shots on target by Arsenal but Mikel Arteta's side excelled defensively.

Player ratings Brighton: Verbruggen (6), Wieffer (7), Boscagli (6), Van Hecke (7), Kadioglu (7), Baleba (6), Gross (6), Hinshelwood (6), Gomez (6), Rutter (7), Mitoma (6)



Subs: Minteh (7), Welbeck (6), Howell (6), Veltman (6), Ayari (6)



Chelsea: Raya (8), Timber (8), Mosquera (7), Gabriel (9), Hincapie (8); Zubimendi (6), Rice (7), Eze (6); Saka (6), Gyokeres (5), Martinelli (6)



Subs: Havertz (6), Trossard (6), Calafiori (6), Norgaard (6)



Player of the Match: Gabriel Magalhaes

Brighton dominated most of the game, registering 60 per cent of the possession, but Arsenal, led by defenders Gabriel and Piero Hincapie, and without the injured William Saliba, could not be breached.

Brighton's best chance came after only two minutes, when David Raya sent a pass straight to Baleba, whose attempted chip had to be cleared off the line by Gabriel.

They pinned Arsenal in their own half for long periods, but only mustered a further two shots on target, with Raya saving well from Georginio Rutter as the home fans became increasingly frustrated by perceived time-wasting from the visitors.

Arsenal had a chance to put the game beyond Brighton when substitute Kai Havertz had a diagonal effort saved by Verbruggen, but their slender lead meant they had to survive a nervy finale.

The final whistle, and news of Manchester City's draw with Forest, prompted wild celebrations from the travelling fans as Arsenal opened up a seven-point gap, albeit having played a game more.
